rulings.Case Title: ERIKA MCCARTNEY VS. FOODS ALIVE INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions; Points Of Authorities; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted, no opposition filed. Defendants to serve discovery responses without objections within ten days. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,040. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)

Defendants Sunrise Carlisle LP and the Sunrise Senior Living Management, Inc.'s demurrer to the fifth and sixth causes of action for fraud and fraudulent accounting in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff The Carlisle of San Francisco Homeowners Association is overruled as to both causes of action. Liberally construed, both claims adequately allege actionable fraud by concealment claims. Defendants can seek more information about the claims by discovery and, if warranted, can file a summary motion that the claims lack merit. rulings.Case Title: CUI YING ZHOU VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action for dangerous condition of public property and granted as to the second, third and fourth causes of action for vicarious liability for employee, vicarious liability for independent contractor, and failure to discharge mandatory duty. The City's argument that natural condition immunity bars plaintiffs' claim for dangerous condition of public property lacks merit. There is a disputed issue whether the Canary Island pine tree is a "natural condition." The evidence demonstrates that the City planted the tree in 1957 or 1958 and it is not native to California. (Cloyd Dec., Ex. 3 (the City's response to plaintiff's special rog. 7) and Leggitt Dec. pars. 9-10.) The evidence further shows that the City planted the tree in a non-natural fashion and "up-limbed" it, which contributed to the danger. (Leggitt Dec. pars. 45-46 and 60.) There is also a triable issue whether the property where the injury occurred-a developed urban park-constitutes an "unimproved public property" within the meaning of the statute. (County of San Mateo v. Sup. Crt. (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 724; the City's UMF 4-5; plaintiffs' additional facts 11-13.) The City's argument that trail immunity bars the dangerous condition claim also lacks merit. Government Code 831.4 is inapplicable because there are no allegations that Ms. Zhou was harmed based on the condition, location, or design of the trail. (Toeppe v. City of San Diego (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 921, 931.) In addition, the record is unclear precisely where she was injured and if she was even on the "trail." (City's UMF 1.) The City's objections to the Leggitt declaration are overruled. (Leggitt Dec. pars. 46, 56-57, 60, 62-64, and 77.) There is a triable dispute whether the City created the dangerous condition of public property. (Leggitt Dec. par. 60) A reasonable trier of fact could also conclude that the City had actual or constructive notice of the danger through its prior pruning efforts. (Leggitt Dec., par. 63.) The City is entitled to summary adjudication on the second and third causes of action because plaintiffs do not oppose the motion as to these claims and their remedy is to pursue the dangerous condition of public property claim. The City is entitled to summary adjudication on the fourth cause of action court because plaintiffs do not oppose summary adjudication and the San Francisco Environment Code 1200 et seq. is "advisory," not mandatory. (B.H. v. County of San Bernardino (2015) 62 Cal.4th 168, 180; San Francisco Environment Code 1201(d).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: OREGON MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. BRUCE I. GILBERT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatory No. 4.1 From Plaintiff 3300-3308 Mission St. Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT BRUCE GILBERT, BRUCE I. GILBERT AS TRUSTEE, WEST COAST PROPERTY MANAGEMENT Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatory No. 4.1 From Plaintiff 3300-3308 Mission St. LLC. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Plaintiff 3300 will provide a complete answer to Form Interrogatory No. 4.1 within ten (10) days. Discovery is broadly permitted and insurance information is discoverable even if ultimately not admissible under the collateral source rule. See Norton v. Superior Court (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1750, 1760-62. The Court finds that it cannot determine prior to trial whether the collateral source rule will bar all evidence concerning insurance or that the information categorically will not lead to the discovery of admissible evidence or otherwise discoverable information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/HK)

rulings.Case Title: YA-HSIANG CHEN HU VS. YOU HAO CHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2018, Line 14, DEFENDANT UNIVERSAL PARAGON CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Universal Paragon Corporation's demurrer to the first, fourth through seventh and ninth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Ya-Hsiang Chen Hu is overruled as to the fifth cause of action for declaratory relief and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all other causes of action alleged against UPC. Liberally construed, the FAC alleges a dispute warranting a declaration from the court determining who owns the stock in UPC and UPC is an appropriate party to such a declaratory relief claims. All other claims alleged against UPC are confusing about the factual and legal bases for asserted liability against UPC. If he can do so in good faith, plaintiff is given leave to clarify those claims by alleging ultimate facts that support liability against UPC and specifying on what basis (e.g. agency, alter ego, promoter, own misconduct) liability is sought to be established against UPC for each claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for UPC is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: INTERVISION SYSTEMS, LLC VS. BEN ESCOBAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANTA BEN ESCOBAR, AMIR FOULADGAR, ORLANDO LUCERO, AND EVOTEK, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants Ben Escobar, Amir Fouladgar, Orlando Lucero and Evotek, Inc.'s motion to strike references to the Orange County case in the complaint filed by plaintiff InterVision Systems, LLC is denied. Defendants have not shown on this pleading motion that the allegations regarding the Orange County case are irrelevant or legally prejudicial to them. This ruling is merely a decision at this stage of the case not to strike the references to the Orange County case in the complaint. It is not, and should not be taken, as an indication on how the court would rule on other matters pertaining to the Orange County case, such as discovery or admissibility at trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA ** DOUGLAS D. STURGEON ET AL VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue. Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to Santa Clara Superior Court and all costs of transfer are to be paid by plaintiff Douglas Sturgeon. Per CCP 395(b), San Francisco is not a proper venue for this case. Ford may not have this case transferred to Solano County since that county is not where it has its principal place of business in California. Since Santa Clara County is where Ford has designated as its principal place of business in California, Ford's alternative request for transfer to that county is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ford is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
